description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each with a natural wood grain texture, and is reinforced by two wide, dark metal hoops encircling the upper and lower sections. The top surface is flat and appears to be made from the same wood as the staves, tightly fitted to form a sealed lid. The wood has a light brown color with subtle variations, while the metal bands are matte black, providing both structural integrity and visual contrast. There are no visible handles, but one view shows small round metal rivets or plugs on the bands, likely for securing the hoops. The overall appearance suggests durability and a traditional design suitable for storage or decorative use in urban environments.
description: A traditional wooden barrel with light brown staves and two matte black metal hoops, featuring a flat wooden lid and no visible handles.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from the front, showing the full curvature of the staves and both metal hoops, with no visible rivets or plugs.
description_view_1: This view is similar to the front, highlighting the smooth wood grain and the seamless fit of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: This side view reveals small round metal rivets or plugs on the metal hoops, adding detail to the reinforcement.
description_view_3: The barrel is shown from the opposite side, with a clear view of the wood texture and uninterrupted metal bands.
